title: cuphea lobophora var. occidentalis
intro: Cuphea lobophora var. occidentalis, also known as the Mexican Heather, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and treat respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Mexican Heather typically involve infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water to make teas or using the plant's extracts in topical creams and ointments.
